titleStmt.title: SII 31.231: original edition by C. A. Padmanabha Sastri and T. S. Ravishankar
No. 231. (A.R. No. B 297 of 1936.) ANANTĀPURAM, JAMMALAMADUGU TALUK, CUDDAPAH DISTRICT.

summary.p: Slab used as a mortar near the temple of Narasiṁhasvāmi about two miles to the south-west of the village.
It is dated in the Śaka 1635 (wrong for 1639), Hēmaḷambi, Vaiśākha śu. 2, Aṁggāraka vāra (Tuesday) corresponding to 1717 A.D. April 2.

It states that the stone mortar (rōlu) was caused to be made by Nārayana, the son of [ā*]chāri Karamala Chinamārasimbu, who is the son of Venkataya and Venkaṭayya described as the son of Nārāyaṇa, the Karaṇam of Anantāpuram.

encodingDesc.p: Encoded in TEI/EpiDoc according to the DHARMA Encoding Guide for Documentary Editions, version 2

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīrāmā . śālivāhana śakavaruṣaṁbulu veyiṁnni
Aruṁnūṭamu ptaAdu 1635(9) Agunēṁṭi hēma
ḷaṁbbi saṁvatsara vaiśākha śu 2 Aṁggāraka vāraṁnāḍu
Anaṁttapuraṁ karaṇaṁ nārāyaṇa komāruḍu
veṁkaṭaya[Ā]cāri karamalacina nārasiṁhvuni
komāruḍu nārāyaṇa cātanu ceyiṁccina rō
lu . Iṁduku sākṣulu pogaṭūri Agasāla veṁ
kaṭādri komāruḍu pedayya Aṁnyaṁ mōrraya komā
ruḍu ya vīrula niṁmatini sōhastaṁ veṁkaṭavrālu .
Digital edition of SII 31.231 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
